title: Ulceby Cross Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot Flg Off. Nicholas Agnew Ansdell

external_narratives.summary: Commonwealth War Graves Commission story page for Flying Officer Nicholas Agnew Ansdell (1924-1945), pilot of Lancaster PB476. Details his service with 12 Squadron and the circumstances of the loss during the Luftwaffe's Operation Gisela.
external_narratives.key_details: Ansdell was RAFVR service number 150291. The Lancaster was shot down at 00:29 hours on 4 March 1945 by a Luftwaffe intruder during Operation Gisela — the last major German air operation of the war.

external_narratives.summary: International war sites database entry for the Lancaster PB476 memorial at Ulceby Cross. Records the location of the memorial stone and the crew details.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial is catalogued in the international Traces of War database. Crew: Fg Off Ansdell (Pilot), Fg Off Heath (Air Bomber), Fg Off Hunter (Navigator), Sgt Mellor (Air Gunner), Sgt Parry (Wireless Operator), Sgt Shafer (Flight Engineer), Sgt Walker (Air Gunner).

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the memorial to the crew of Lancaster PB476 at Ulceby Cross, Lincolnshire. Catalogued as memorial reference 20271.
external_narratives.key_details: Formally registered in the IWM national war memorials database.

external_narratives.summary: Lincolnshire County Council heritage record for the memorial to Lancaster PB476 at Ulceby Cross. Notes the memorial was unveiled in 1995 on land near the crash site.
external_narratives.key_details: Monument reference MLI125556. Memorial unveiled 1995. The Lancaster dived almost vertically into the ground with such force that the engines were found buried 10 feet deep.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
